frame-hub.oneLiner: A full-featured, community-driven Warframe build planner with real-time stat and damage calculations across frames, weapons, companions, and more.
frame-hub.overview: Frame Hub (frame-hub.com) is a web-based Warframe build planner that lets players mod and optimize warframes, weapons, companions, Archguns, Archwings, Necramechs, and Railjacks with real-time stat calculations, Archon Shard slots, arcanes, and a full mod/arcane codex using in-game card artwork. It includes a damage simulator that models level scaling, armor, and elemental combos, a riven generator/grader with disposition-adjusted ranges, side-by-side build and loadout comparison, and a community section for browsing, upvoting, filtering, and loading shared builds. It offers player sync that pulls active Arsenal data from Warframe, and accounts (sign-in) for saving and sharing loadouts. Per the site, it is maintained by a solo developer in their spare time, with a Discord community and optional donation support.

Damage simulator with enemy level scaling, armor, and elemental combos

Note that many search hits for "Frame Hub"/"FrameHub" refer to unrelated tools (a mastery checklist at framehub.paroxity.net, WFCD's warframe-hub, and warframehub.com recruitment), which makes isolating genuine feedback difficult.

Its optional 'player sync' pulls public Arsenal/profile data (the same public Warframe profile data other fan tools use) rather than logging into your account. Standard practice for this class of tool and within DE's fan-content norms.

frame-hub.tierReason: It is a genuinely full-featured build planner (arguably broader in scope than many competitors, covering Railjack, Necramech, and rivens), but external adoption and community footprint could not be verified and appear small relative to established tools like Overframe. Useful as an alternative or for its niche builders, hence situational rather than recommended/essential.

semlar-comp.oneLiner: A free web tool from Semlar.com that lets Warframe players compare two riven rolls side by side to decide which is better.
semlar-comp.overview: Semlar's Riven Comparator (semlar.com/comp) is one of several riven utilities on Semlar.com, a long-running suite of community-made Warframe tools. The comparator is designed to "Compare Rolls," letting a player evaluate two versions of a riven mod against each other — useful when deciding whether a fresh re-roll is an upgrade over the current one. It sits alongside Semlar's better-known riven tools: the Riven Calculator (stat ranges), the Riven Price Guide, and Riven Hunter/trade search. It is a browser-based, read-only reference tool that does not touch the game client.

Access to stat-range data to judge how good each stat is within its possible range
Price-history data to inform value comparisons

Covers riven-capable weapons across the game
semlar-comp.bestFor: Riven traders and rollers who want to quickly judge whether one riven roll is objectively better than another before keeping, selling, or re-rolling.
semlar-comp.sentimentSummary: Direct external discussion of the Comparator specifically is sparse, but Semlar.com as a whole is a well-established and trusted name in the Warframe riven community, frequently cited alongside its widely used Riven Calculator and Price Guide. The comparator is a smaller, niche sub-tool within that respected suite rather than a standalone tool with its own large following. No significant criticism of the tool itself was found.

wfm-price-history.oneLiner: A browser extension that embeds price-history charts, forecasts, and trade signals directly into warframe.market item pages.
wfm-price-history.overview: WFM Price History is a Chrome/Firefox extension (Manifest V3) by developer FireZenk/OptimusRex that augments warframe.market item pages with in-line analytics instead of requiring separate tabs or tools. It renders a toggleable 90-day and 48-hour SVG price chart with a min/max band and daily volume bars, plus a 7-day linear-regression forecast, buy/sell signals from 90-day averages, volatility scoring, a 1-10 liquidity rating, and optimal trading-hour hints. It also offers a standalone dashboard with search, history, CSV export, notes, a price-alert watchlist with desktop notifications, and Prime set-vs-parts arbitrage/ducat comparisons. All data comes from warframe.market's public API and calculations run client-side; it stores watchlists locally and collects no personal data.
keyFeatures: Toggleable 90-day / 48-hour SVG price chart with min/max band and daily volume bars
7-day price forecast via linear regression with confidence intervals
Buy/sell trading signals based on 90-day averages
Volatility score and 1-10 liquidity rating
Optimal trading-hour recommendations
Watchlist with price-threshold desktop alert notifications
Standalone dashboard with search, history, CSV export and personal notes
Prime set-vs-parts arbitrage and ducat comparison analysis
wfm-price-history.bestFor: Warframe.market traders who want price-history charts, forecasts and liquidity/volatility metrics embedded directly on item pages rather than switching to a separate analytics site.
wfm-price-history.sentimentSummary: There is very little public community discussion of this extension. The only quantified feedback is its Chrome Web Store rating of 3.9/5 from 11 ratings; the Firefox listing has no reviews, and no Reddit threads, YouTube videos, or forum posts about it could be verified. Adoption is small (roughly 882 Chrome users plus 29 Firefox users, and 7 GitHub stars), and the mixed-leaning 3.9 store score suggests it works but has not achieved strong consensus.

safety.notes: Read-only web tool: it only reads warframe.market's public API and injects UI into the site's pages in the browser. It does not read game memory, is not an in-game overlay, and requires no Warframe or warframe.market login credentials. Minimal permissions (storage, notifications, alarms), watchlists stored locally, and no personal-data collection per the listings. No RMT involvement. This is the kind of read-only external tool DE generally tolerates.

wfm-price-history.tierReason: Feature-rich and functional with a clean read-only design, but adoption is small (~882 Chrome + ~29 Firefox users, 7 GitHub stars) and community feedback is sparse with a middling 3.9/5 store rating, so it serves a small audience rather than being widely relied upon.
pros: Puts full price-history charts, forecasts and volume data right on warframe.market item pages, no tab-switching
Genuinely useful trader metrics: volatility, liquidity score, optimal trading hours, set-vs-parts arbitrage
Privacy-friendly and safe: public-API only, local storage, no login, minimal permissions, MIT-licensed open source
Cross-browser (Chrome and Firefox) with a standalone dashboard, CSV export and price alerts
Actively maintained (v1.7.0, updated July 2026) with a roadmap of planned features

Chrome Web Store rating is only 3.9/5 (11 ratings), indicating mixed reception
Forecasts/signals are simple linear-regression heuristics and should not be treated as reliable predictions
No verifiable Reddit, YouTube or forum coverage, so little community validation

MIT-licensed open-source repo, Manifest V3, latest release v1.7.0 (June 30 2026). 6 open issues, all feature enhancements (portfolio tracker, trade log, item comparison, extended time ranges) targeting v2.0.0 - no visible bug reports.

de-worldstate.oneLiner: Digital Extremes' official raw world-state JSON endpoint that feeds live game data (alerts, invasions, sorties, Void Trader, events) to nearly every third-party Warframe tool.
de-worldstate.overview: The "DE World State Protocol" refers to Warframe's official worldState.php endpoint (https://api.warframe.com/cdn/worldState.php), a public read-only JSON feed published directly by Digital Extremes. It returns the live state of the game world: active alerts, invasions, sorties/Archon hunts, fissures, syndicate missions, the Void Trader (Baro Ki'Teer) manifest, community/nightwave events, flash sales, in-game market data, plus metadata like build label, world seed, and generation timestamp. It is a raw, unauthenticated data source rather than an end-user app, and it is the upstream foundation that community APIs (most notably WFCD's warframe-worldstate-parser and warframestat.us) parse and re-serve to power timers, alert trackers, and companion apps.
keyFeatures: Official first-party data source published directly by Digital Extremes
Returns comprehensive live world state as JSON
Includes Void Trader / Baro Ki'Teer manifest with prices
Covers events, community goals, flash sales, and in-game market landing pages
Multilingual localized strings (en, fr, ja, zh, ko, etc.)
Metadata: build label/version, world seed, and generation timestamp
Unauthenticated, read-only public HTTPS endpoint
Raw internal format (MongoDB ObjectIDs, epoch timestamps) requiring client-side parsing
de-worldstate.bestFor: Developers building Warframe companion tools, bots, and trackers who want the authoritative upstream data rather than a third-party mirror; not intended for non-technical end users.
de-worldstate.sentimentSummary: Direct external sentiment sources (Reddit/YouTube/Trustpilot) could not be gathered this session because the web-search budget was exhausted before discovery, so no community quotes are reported here. What is verifiable is that this endpoint is the de facto standard upstream for the Warframe developer ecosystem: the WFCD warframe-worldstate-parser openly documents fetching from it, and warframestat.us is built on parsing it. Its practical reputation among developers is as reliable and canonical, with the main friction being that the raw format is dense and undocumented officially, which is exactly why community parsers exist.

safety.notes: Read-only, unauthenticated public JSON endpoint served by Digital Extremes over HTTPS. It reads no game memory, requires no login or credentials, and is not an overlay, so it raises none of DE's overlay/memory-reading or account-security concerns. It is DE's own first-party data. Only practical caveats are polite rate-limiting/caching (the feed updates frequently) and that the raw schema is unofficial and can change without notice.

de-worldstate.tierReason: As the authoritative, first-party upstream that powers essentially the entire third-party Warframe tooling ecosystem (warframestat.us, worldstate parsers, alert/timer apps), it is heavily relied upon by developers. It is not tier 'essential' as a standalone product because it is a raw developer endpoint, not a finished tool for players, and most people consume it indirectly through parsers.
pros: Authoritative first-party data straight from Digital Extremes with no intermediary
Free, public, and unauthenticated
Extremely comprehensive coverage of live game state
The canonical upstream that nearly all community tools depend on
Reliable uptime as it is production infrastructure serving the live game
cons: Raw, dense format with no official public documentation
Uses internal encodings (MongoDB ObjectIDs, epoch-ms) that require parsing to be usable
Schema can change without notice or versioning guarantees
Not usable by non-developers without a parser/frontend
No official support channel or SLA for third-party consumers

otherReviews.snippet: Community (Warframe Community Developers) open-source parser that fetches https://api.warframe.com/cdn/worldState.php and converts the raw response into usable JavaScript objects; used in production by warframestat.us APIs across PC/PS/Xbox/Switch.

wfm-overlay.oneLiner: A Python OCR overlay that reads item names off your Warframe screen and shows live warframe.market prices in-game.
wfm-overlay.overview: Warframe-Market-Overlay is an open-source (GPL-3.0) Python desktop overlay that uses Tesseract OCR to read item names displayed on your Warframe screen, then queries the warframe.market API to show top seller prices, averages, and ducat values in movable in-game windows. It is triggered by hotkeys (Ctrl+Shift+F to scan, Ctrl+Shift+H to toggle) and supports resizable/saveable layouts and UI scaling for 4K. It is a small hobby project by developer JonathanSourdough, functional but described as not fully optimized. The repository has had no code pushed since December 2022.
keyFeatures: Real-time on-screen item scanning via Tesseract OCR
Live warframe.market pricing: top seller prices, averages, ducat values
Hotkey-driven (Ctrl+Shift+F scan, Ctrl+Shift+H toggle visibility)
Movable/resizable info and search windows
Save/load multiple layouts
UI scaling up to 2x for 4K monitors
WTS/WTB (sell vs buy) listing-type selection
wfm-overlay.bestFor: Warframe traders on Windows who are comfortable installing Python and Tesseract and want an in-game price overlay without alt-tabbing to warframe.market.
wfm-overlay.sentimentSummary: No external community discussion (Reddit, YouTube, Trustpilot, Product Hunt) could be located within this research session, so sentiment cannot be honestly characterized from user feedback. The only measurable signal is GitHub adoption, which is modest: 37 stars, 2 forks, and 4 open issues, with no commits since December 2022, indicating a small niche audience and an effectively dormant project.

safety.notes: This is a third-party in-game overlay. It reads the screen via OCR (Tesseract) rather than reading or injecting into game memory, which is the less risky category and closer to what DE has generally tolerated for community trading tools (similar in approach to widely used tools like WFInfo). It does not require warframe.market login or account credentials and only queries the public market API. Standard caution applies to any overlay running over the game, and users should confirm current Warframe ToS compliance. Not RMT. Requires manual install of Python 3.8.2+ and Tesseract OCR from source (GPL-3.0), so users should review the code themselves.

wfm-overlay.tierReason: Small, working hobby project (37 GitHub stars, 2 forks) with no updates since December 2022 and no discoverable community reviews or coverage. Its OCR-overlay niche is dominated by more popular tools, so it appeals to a small audience of self-hosting traders.

otherReviews.rating: 37 stars, 2 forks, 4 open issues; last push Dec 8, 2022
otherReviews.snippet: Repository description: 'This is an overlay written in Python for the purpose of reading the screen in Warframe, and reporting the values from warframe.market associated with that item.' Created April 8, 2020; GPL-3.0; not archived but no commits since Dec 2022.

pros: Free and open source (GPL-3.0) — code is inspectable
Uses screen OCR rather than game-memory reading, the lower-risk overlay approach
No account login or credentials required; only reads the public warframe.market API
Convenient in-game price lookups without alt-tabbing
Configurable layouts and 4K/UI scaling
cons: Effectively unmaintained — no commits since December 2022
Very small adoption (37 stars) and no discoverable reviews or tutorials
Manual setup required: install Python and Tesseract OCR and add to PATH
Developer notes it is functional but not fully optimized
Windows-focused; OCR accuracy depends on resolution/settings
Standard third-party overlay caution applies to Warframe ToS

relic-finder.oneLiner: A free, web-based Warframe reference site that maps relics to prime parts with drop rates, farming guides, and a personal relic tracker.
relic-finder.overview: The Relic Finder (warframerelics.com) is a fan-made, browser-based resource that catalogs Warframe relics (Lith, Meso, Neo, Axi, Requiem), the prime parts they drop, and per-part drop-rate percentages. Beyond the database it offers farming/location guides, a relic tracker for monitoring your own collection, Prime Access history/availability data, and Baro Ki'Teer and alert tracking. It is entirely web-based and read-only: no account login and no reading of game memory. It is not affiliated with Digital Extremes and positions itself as a simpler, more focused alternative to the official Warframe Wiki.
keyFeatures: Relic database organized by era (Lith, Meso, Neo, Axi, Requiem)
Drop-rate percentages for each prime part
Relic-to-prime-part relationship mapping
Personal relic tracker to monitor your collection
Farming guides and location recommendations
Prime Access history and current availability data
Baro Ki'Teer tracker and live alerts
Editorial guidance combining game data with player experience
relic-finder.bestFor: Newer or intermediate players who want a simpler, more focused view of which relics drop which prime parts and where to farm them, without navigating the denser official Wiki.
relic-finder.sentimentSummary: No external community discussion of this specific tool could be verified. Reddit, YouTube, Trustpilot, and other feedback sources could not be retrieved in this session (web search budget exhausted and Reddit/Trustpilot access blocked), and no threads, videos, or reviews were confirmed. The only substantive information comes from the site's own pages, so community reception is effectively unknown.

safety.notes: Web-based, read-only reference site. No account login, no credentials collected, and per its own pages it uses editorial/manual data rather than reading game memory, so it poses no overlay or account-risk concerns under DE's policies. It is a fan-made site not affiliated with Digital Extremes. No RMT involvement observed.

relic-finder.tierReason: No verifiable evidence of adoption or community discussion (no Reddit, YouTube, or review presence confirmed). It duplicates data already well-served by the official Wiki, WFCD drop tables, and in-game relic UIs, so it appears to serve a small audience rather than being a widely-relied-upon tool.
pros: Completely free with no login required
Focused, beginner-friendly layout compared to the official Wiki

Read-only web tool with no account or game-memory access risk

wf-relic-tracker.oneLiner: A browser-based reference tool for tracking Warframe Prime relics, their vault status, and refinement-tier drop rates.
wf-relic-tracker.overview: Warframe Relic Tracker (wfrelictracker.com) is a free web app that lets players monitor all Prime relics across the Lith, Meso, Neo, Axi, and Requiem eras. It displays each relic's vault status (obtainable vs. vaulted), live drop data, and refinement-tier information to help players decide which relics to farm and run for specific Prime part rewards. It is a read-only browser reference rather than a download, overlay, or memory-reading client, so it carries no in-game or account risk.
keyFeatures: Tracks all Prime relics across Lith, Meso, Neo, Axi, and Requiem eras
Shows vault status (obtainable vs. vaulted) per relic
Live drop data display
Refinement-tier (Intact/Exceptional/Flawless/Radiant) optimization info
Aya rotation tracker
Manual data refresh
Runs entirely in the browser with no download or login
wf-relic-tracker.bestFor: Warframe players who want a quick, browser-based reference for which Prime relics are currently obtainable or vaulted and how to optimize refinement tiers when farming Prime parts.
wf-relic-tracker.sentimentSummary: No community discussion of this specific tool could be found. Searches across Reddit, YouTube, and general web engines surfaced no threads, videos, reviews, or forum posts referencing wfrelictracker.com, and it does not appear on Trustpilot or app stores. It appears to be a small, relatively unknown web tool with little to no measurable adoption footprint at the time of research.

wf-relic-tracker.tierReason: Functionally a safe, free relic/vault reference, but no evidence of community adoption, reviews, or discussion was found, and established alternatives (WFCD-based tools, Warframe Wiki, wf.market) dominate this space. reframed.oneLiner: A web-based Warframe build creator and community loadout browser for Warframes, weapons, and companions.
reframed.overview: RE:FRAMED (reframed.site) is an unofficial, fan-made web tool for creating, sharing, and discovering Warframe builds. Users can build loadouts for Warframes, primary/secondary/melee weapons, and companions (Sentinels, Kubrows, Kavats, MOAs), browse a community-shared build library, and follow in-game news like hotfixes and Prime Access. It positions itself as a lighter, faster alternative to navigating multiple resources, in the same space as Overframe. It explicitly states it is not affiliated with Digital Extremes.

Community build library with explore, filter and sort (builds?tab=explore)
Warframe roster database with per-frame build access
Weapon database covering primaries, secondaries and melee

User profiles for saving/tracking builds
In-app news feed for hotfixes and Prime Access
Quick search (Ctrl+K) and Discord community
reframed.bestFor: Warframe players who want a clean, modern web interface to create, save, and browse community builds for frames, weapons, and companions without using the in-game arsenal or older tools.

arsenyx.oneLiner: A comprehensive, actively maintained web-based Warframe build planner covering the full arsenal of frames, weapons, companions, and mechs.
arsenyx.overview: Arsenyx (arsenyx.com) is a web-based Warframe build planner that lets players plan and organize loadouts across the game's full arsenal. Its sitemap exposes roughly 1,260 item pages spanning 152 Warframes (including Prime variants), 342 primary weapons, 426 melee weapons, 222 secondary weapons, 40 companion weapons, 35 exalted weapons, 38 Archwings, 3 Necramechs, 40 companions, and Railjack Plexus. The site also has builds, organizations ("orgs"), docs, changelog, and a public API section, and its pages carried very recent lastmod dates (2026-07-16), indicating active maintenance. Because the front end is a JavaScript single-page app, deeper feature text (login requirements, damage calculation, sharing specifics) could not be verified from the raw HTML.
keyFeatures: Build planning across the full Warframe arsenal (frames, primaries, secondaries, melee, companions)
Coverage of Prime, Kuva/Tenet and other variants
Support for Archwings, Necramechs, exalted/companion weapons and Railjack Plexus
Organizations / team ('orgs') feature for group build management
Public API with developer documentation
Changelog indicating active, ongoing development (item pages updated 2026-07-16)
Web-based tool requiring no client install
arsenyx.bestFor: Warframe players who want a modern, browser-based build planner with broad item coverage and a public API, plus groups wanting shared/organization builds.
arsenyx.sentimentSummary: No community sentiment could be verified for Arsenyx. Reddit, YouTube, Trustpilot, and other feedback sources could not be searched (web search budget exhausted this session) and none were confirmed. The tool itself presents as a legitimate, comprehensive and actively maintained build planner, but its real-world adoption and reception are unknown from the evidence gathered.

safety.notes: Web-based build planner that operates in the browser; no evidence it reads game memory, runs an in-game overlay, or requires Warframe account credentials, so it does not raise DE overlay/ToS concerns. It is a build-planning/reference site, not an RMT or trading marketplace. Any account/login is presumably its own site account for saving builds, not Warframe credentials.

arsenyx.tierReason: The tool looks well-built, comprehensive, and actively maintained, but no external adoption or community sentiment evidence could be found, and it competes in a space dominated by established planners (Overframe, Warframe Builder). Without demonstrated reach it is rated niche rather than recommended.

knightframe.oneLiner: KnightFrame is a Warframe build planner and loadout-sharing database run by the Knights of Aeterna clan community.
knightframe.overview: KnightFrame is the build-planning and loadout-sharing section of knightsaeterna.com, the community hub of the "Knights of Aeterna" Warframe clan/alliance (established September 2025). It lets players browse and create Warframe builds and loadouts, with the site describing itself as offering "top, recent, and expert loadouts from Knights of Aeterna operatives." It sits alongside a broader toolset on the same site (damage calculator, credit calculator, Riven grader, market tracker, world-state info, LFG). Viewing builds is free and open; creating builds requires logging in (Discord login integration is present).
keyFeatures: Warframe build/loadout creation and sharing
Community leaderboards for top and recent builds
Weapon damage calculator (same site)
Riven grading tool
In-game market tracker and world-state info
Looking-For-Group (LFG) system
Free to browse; Discord login to create builds
Part of a clan hub with ~2600+ players across an allied network
knightframe.bestFor: Members and prospective members of the Knights of Aeterna clan/alliance who want a shared place to browse and post Warframe loadouts, plus casual players looking for an all-in-one clan community site with builds and utility tools.
knightframe.sentimentSummary: No external community discussion of KnightFrame could be located. It is a very new (site established September 2025), clan-specific tool with no discoverable Reddit threads, YouTube coverage, store listings, or review-site presence at the time of research. All available information comes from the tool's own website, so community sentiment is effectively unknown.

It serves the Knights of Aeterna community rather than the broader Warframe playerbase, and mainstream build planners (Overframe, WarframeBuilder) dominate that space, so its audience is small.

webutilitykit-riven.oneLiner: A free, web-based tracker that charts real-time and historical market prices for unrolled Warframe Riven mods using Warframe Market data.
webutilitykit-riven.overview: Riven Price Tracker (part of WebUtilityKit Lab) is a client-facing web page that monitors current and historical prices for unrolled Riven mods per weapon, pulling from the Warframe Market (WFM) public API. It shows bottom/lowest prices, price-history charts across multiple intervals, a ranking of the most valuable rivens, and per-weapon pages, computing value from a weighted average of the top-5 lowest in-game sell orders. It is built entirely on Cloudflare's edge stack (Workers, Durable Objects, D1, KV) and offered in both English and Chinese. The tool was created by developer "jiachaoDing" for a roommate who plays Warframe, is currently feature-frozen, and is maintained as a free long-term resource.
keyFeatures: Real-time lowest/bottom price per weapon from Warframe Market data
Historical price-history charts (project page cites 7/30/90-day analysis)
Ranking of the top ~50 most valuable Riven mods by lowest sell price
Per-weapon search and dedicated weapon pages
Value calculated from weighted average of the 5 lowest in-game sell orders
Automated periodic data collection (top-50 weapons refresh more often than the long tail)
Bilingual English/Chinese interface
Edge-hosted for fast, instant-loading pages (Cloudflare Workers/D1/KV)
webutilitykit-riven.bestFor: Traders who want a quick, no-login web view of unrolled Riven mod bottom prices and price trends per weapon, particularly players comfortable with a lightweight single-purpose tool over full-featured trading sites.
webutilitykit-riven.sentimentSummary: There is essentially no public English-language community discussion of this specific tool. It gained modest organic traction (~1,550 unique visitors as of late January 2026) after the creator's roommate posted a promotional video on the Chinese gaming platform Xiaoheihe, but no Reddit threads, YouTube reviews, Trustpilot pages, or Product Hunt listings about it could be found. Sentiment cannot be meaningfully assessed from external evidence; it appears to be a small, functional niche tool rather than a widely adopted one.

safety.notes: Web-based, read-only tool that only reads the public Warframe Market API. No game-memory access, no overlay, no login/credentials, and no real-money trading involved, so it poses no account risk under DE's rules.

webutilitykit-riven.tierReason: Functional and safe, but tiny audience (~1,550 visitors), feature-frozen, no English community footprint, and it overlaps with far more established riven tools (Warframe Market, Riven Mirror, RivenRadar). Useful for a specific quick-lookup need but not broadly adopted.

relicarium.oneLiner: A lightweight web reference tool for looking up where to farm Warframe prime items, relics, and equipment.

It is a solo hobby project by a developer credited as "LL" (Steam/Reddit handle llxd), supports English and Brazilian Portuguese, and has no official affiliation with Digital Extremes.

droptable-wf.oneLiner: A web-based, searchable Warframe drop-table lookup for finding where items, Prime parts, relics, and Rivens drop.
droptable-wf.overview: droptable.wf is a third-party web resource that lets Warframe players search the game's drop tables to quickly find which missions, enemies, and activities yield a given item. It covers standard drop data across mission types (Defense, Survival, Spy, Disruption, Excavation, and others), Prime/relic lookups for prime equipment, and Riven mod searches. It functions as a faster, more searchable alternative to Digital Extremes' official drop-table reference, which it links to as the authoritative source.
keyFeatures: Searchable drop-table database across many mission types
Prime and relic lookup for prime equipment parts
Riven mod search by weapon
Reverse lookup: find where a specific item drops
Links out to the official DE droptable reference
Browser-based, no install or login required
droptable-wf.bestFor: Players who want to quickly look up where an item, Prime part, relic, or Riven drops without manually scanning DE's raw official drop tables.
droptable-wf.sentimentSummary: No external community discussion (Reddit, YouTube, Trustpilot, Product Hunt, or forums) could be verified in this research pass, as the web-search budget was exhausted before those queries ran. The tool itself presents as a straightforward, read-only drop-table lookup. It appears to be a smaller/newer entrant in a space already served by well-known resources like the official DE drop tables, the Warframe Wiki, and WFCD-based tools, so adoption evidence is currently unconfirmed.

safety.notes: Read-only informational website. No account login, no game-memory reading, no overlay, and no real-money trading. Does not interact with the game client, so it carries no ToS or account-security risk. Standard caution applies to any third-party site (verify drop data against DE's official droptables, which the site itself links to as the source of truth).

droptable-wf.tierReason: A functional, safe drop-lookup site, but it competes directly with the entrenched official DE drop tables, the Warframe Wiki, and WFCD-powered tools. No verifiable adoption or community sentiment was found, so it reads as a small-audience alternative rather than an essential or widely-used tool.
pros: Free, browser-based, no install or account required
Searchable/reverse lookup is faster than scanning DE's raw drop tables
Covers Prime/relic and Riven searches in addition to mission drops
Safe: read-only, no game-memory or credential access
Links to the official DE droptable reference as the authoritative source
cons: No verifiable community adoption or reviews found
Overlaps heavily with established tools (official DE tables, Warframe Wiki, WFCD tools)
Creator/maintainer and data-update cadence not clearly disclosed on the site
Drop data is only as current as its underlying source; accuracy after game updates is unverified

warframe-drops-pwa.oneLiner: A lightweight, installable web app that finds the best place to farm any Warframe item using Digital Extremes' official drop tables.
warframe-drops-pwa.overview: Warframe Drops PWA (by developer farhadrezvani) is a free, open-source Progressive Web App that lets players search for any in-game item and see the best farming locations, sorted by drop chance, based on the official drop tables published by Digital Extremes. Its build process automatically downloads and parses the current official drop data into JSON, and the app is installable on supported browsers for offline use. It is built with Preact, Vite, and TypeScript and is MIT-licensed with the source hosted on GitHub. It is a read-only reference tool with no login, accounts, or game integration.
keyFeatures: Search any item and see the best farming/drop locations
Results sorted by drop chance (descending) with rarity shown
Data sourced directly from Digital Extremes' official drop tables
Installable PWA with offline support
Auto-generated data via build step (downloads + parses latest drop data)
Build metadata with hash + timestamp for data-freshness/integrity
Fast, lightweight Preact/Vite front end
Open-source (MIT) and free
warframe-drops-pwa.bestFor: Players who want a fast, no-frills, installable tool to quickly look up where to farm a specific item from official drop tables, including offline.
warframe-drops-pwa.sentimentSummary: No community discussion (Reddit, YouTube, forums), store ratings, or Trustpilot presence could be found for this tool, and its GitHub repository has only about 4 stars, indicating a very small audience. There is therefore no meaningful body of user sentiment to report. On its technical merits it appears to be a clean, legitimate, read-only reference app sourcing official data, but its adoption is minimal.

safety.notes: Read-only web/PWA that only queries official Digital Extremes drop-table data. No account, credentials, login, or game-memory access; no overlay and no real-money trading. Open-source (MIT) so the code is auditable. Nothing here conflicts with DE's stance on third-party tools.

warframe-drops-pwa.tierReason: A legitimate and safe tool, but with a tiny footprint (~4 GitHub stars, no discoverable community discussion or reviews) and functionality that overlaps heavily with established drop-table resources like the official droptables page, WFCD's drops.warframestat.us, and the Warframe wiki.
pros: Free and open-source (MIT), code is auditable on GitHub
Uses official Digital Extremes drop data, not scraped/guessed values
Installable and works offline
Lightweight and fast (Preact/Vite)
No login, accounts, or account-safety risk
cons: Very small / obscure audience (~4 GitHub stars, no community reviews found)
Overlaps with more established tools (official droptables, WFCD API, wiki)
Single-maintainer hobby project — long-term maintenance not guaranteed
Data is only as current as the last build/deploy of the app
Feature set is narrow (item drop lookup only)

otherReviews.snippet: Official repository: 'a warframe app that finds the best place to farm any in-game item by looking through the official drop tables published by Digital Extremes in a simple UI, installable on supported browsers for offline usage.' MIT license, ~4 stars, 0 forks, 0 open issues, TypeScript/Preact.

warframe-drop-optimizer.oneLiner: A web-based drop-rate and best-mission finder for Warframe farming, branded "Farm Smarter, Not Harder."
warframe-drop-optimizer.overview: Warframe Drop Optimizer (warframedrop.com) is a browser-based farming aid that helps players find the best missions and drop rates for Warframe items. Per its own site description it covers relics, mods, blueprints, and resources with probability/drop-rate analytics, positioning itself as a "farm smarter, not harder" planner. It is a read-only web tool that does not require the game client or account credentials. Beyond the site's own copy, there is very little verifiable public information about who runs it or how widely it is used.
keyFeatures: Search best missions/locations for a given item
Drop-rate and probability data for relics
Coverage of mods, blueprints, and resources
Item-to-mission lookup for farm planning
Browser-based, no download or login required
Positioned as a farming efficiency/optimizer tool
warframe-drop-optimizer.bestFor: Players who want a quick web lookup of where to farm a specific item and its drop odds without opening the in-game or official droptables site.
warframe-drop-optimizer.sentimentSummary: There is essentially no verifiable community sentiment for this specific tool. Extensive searching surfaced no Reddit threads, YouTube reviews, Trustpilot page, Product Hunt listing, or GitHub repo that clearly discuss warframedrop.com. The Warframe drop-data niche is already well served by established options (the official warframe.com/droptables, the community wiki, and drops.warframestat.us), so this tool appears to have a small or unproven audience.

safety.notes: Appears to be a read-only informational website: no game-memory reading, no overlay, no account login or credentials, and no RMT/trading of real money. Using drop-rate lookup sites does not violate Digital Extremes' rules. As with any small third-party site, standard web caution applies, but there is no account-risk or ToS concern evident.

warframe-drop-optimizer.tierReason: The tool exists and offers a legitimate, safe feature set, but no external evidence of meaningful adoption or community endorsement was found, and it competes with well-established, widely trusted drop-data sources. That places it in a small/unproven audience tier rather than recommended.
pros: Free, browser-based, and requires no download or account
Focused specifically on farming efficiency (best mission per item)
Covers multiple item types: relics, mods, blueprints, resources
Read-only and safe from an account/ToS standpoint
cons: Almost no verifiable public reviews, community discussion, or adoption signals
Unclear who maintains it or how current/accurate its data is
Site is a JS-only single-page app with no visible about/creator/attribution info
Overlaps with more established, trusted tools (official droptables, wiki, warframestat.us)

warframe-helper.oneLiner: A free Overwolf overlay/companion by AzerPUG for manually tracking Warframe mastery collection progress across frames, weapons, and companions.
warframe-helper.overview: Warframe Helper is a free, ad-supported Overwolf desktop overlay app made by AzerPUG (a multi-game community/software maker) that serves as a completionist checklist for Warframe. It tracks which Warframes, weapons, and companions you have mastered, lets you sort by availability, vault status, mastery level, and category, and pulls Warframe Market platinum prices for buying/selling. Notably it is a manual tracker — it does not read your in-game inventory automatically, so you tick items off yourself. There is also a companthe web companion at warframe.azerpug.com covering mastery, relics, and resources.
keyFeatures: Mastery completionist checklist for Warframes, Archwings, Necramechs, and vehicles
Weapon tracking: primaries, secondaries, melee
Gildable and companion tracking: KitGuns, Zaws, Amps, Moas, Hounds, Sentinels, Kubrows, Kavats, Vulpaphylas, Predasites
Sort/filter by availability, vault status, mastery level, and category
Warframe Market integration for current platinum buy/sell prices
Companion website (warframe.azerpug.com) with relic and resource databases
Runs as an Overwolf in-game overlay
warframe-helper.bestFor: Warframe players who want a simple visual checklist to plan Mastery Rank progression and see what frames/weapons/companions they still need, and who don't mind updating it manually.
warframe-helper.sentimentSummary: Community feedback is limited and mixed. The Overwolf store rating is 4.3/5 from just 7 reviews across ~21.9K downloads, indicating a small user base. Positive reviews call it useful and say it "works really good," while critics note it is essentially "just a checklist" that "doesn't auto read your in-game stuff" and has a "blinding white" interface. No dedicated Reddit threads, YouTube reviews, or Trustpilot pages discussing this specific tool were found.

safety.notes: Distributed through Overwolf, which vets its apps. It is a manual tracker/overlay and does NOT read Warframe's game memory — the manual data entry is confirmed by a user complaint that it doesn't auto-read in-game data. DE tolerates overlays and companion tools that do not read game memory or automate gameplay; this falls in that acceptable category. No account credentials, no RMT. Only caveat is that it is ad-supported (in-app ads).

warframe-helper.tierReason: Small adoption (~21.9K downloads, only 7 reviews) and a manual, checklist-only approach that competes with more popular auto-tracking tools (e.g. AlecaFrame) and the official Warframe companion app. Useful for a specific audience but not widely discussed or broadly loved.
pros: Free to use
Broad mastery coverage including newer companions like Vulpaphylas and Predasites
Warframe Market price integration for buy/sell decisions
Flexible sorting/filtering by vault status, availability, and mastery
Backed by an established multi-game tool maker (AzerPUG) with a companion website
cons: Manual tracking only — does not auto-read your in-game inventory
Effectively 'just a checklist' per a reviewer
UI criticized as 'blinding white'
Contains in-app ads
Very small user base and little community documentation/reviews
Requires installing the Overwolf platform

otherReviews.snippet: Sample reviews: one user says it 'works really good' and likes the timer lookup; another notes it 'doesn't auto read your ingame stuff,' the interface is 'blinding white,' and it functions essentially as 'a checklist.'

framebuilder.oneLiner: A web-based Warframe build calculator for planning and optimizing Warframe loadouts and mod setups.
framebuilder.overview: FrameBuilder is an unofficial, browser-based Warframe build calculator hosted on Cloudflare Pages (framebuilder.pages.dev). Its page identifies it as a "Warframe Build Calculator," indicating a theory-crafting tool for configuring mods and evaluating build stats. The site is a client-side single-page application, so no additional page content could be crawled, and no external community coverage (Reddit, YouTube, store listings, Trustpilot) could be located within the research budget.
keyFeatures: Web-based Warframe build calculator (no install)
Loadout / mod configuration for Warframes
Stat calculation for theory-crafting builds
Hosted on Cloudflare Pages, free to access
framebuilder.bestFor: Warframe players who want a lightweight, browser-based build planner to experiment with Warframe mod loadouts.
framebuilder.sentimentSummary: No community sentiment could be verified. Web search budget was exhausted before Reddit, YouTube, or other feedback sources could be queried, and the site itself is a JavaScript app exposing only its title. There is no evidence of meaningful adoption or discussion, but also no evidence of complaints or problems.
pros: Free and browser-based, nothing to install
Focused specifically on Warframe build/mod planning
Runs client-side on Cloudflare Pages, so no account or login apparent
cons: No verifiable community adoption or reviews found
Site is a JS SPA with no crawlable/indexable content, limiting discoverability
Feature set could not be independently confirmed beyond 'build calculator'
Unknown maintenance status and data-source freshness

safety.notes: Appears to be a read-only, web-based build calculator running in the browser with no game-memory access, no overlay, and no apparent login or credential collection. This is the low-risk category DE permits (no game memory reading, not an overlay injected into the client). No RMT elements observed. Rating is based on the tool being a standard web planner; specific data-handling claims could not be independently verified.

framebuilder.tierReason: No evidence of meaningful adoption, community discussion, or reviews could be found, and the tool overlaps with well-established build planners (Overframe, Warframe Builder). Absent proof of a wider audience, it reads as a small/obscure project rather than a widely used or essential one.

wondyframe.oneLiner: A lightweight Discord bot that gives Warframe players quick access to relic farming locations, world-state info, and Baro data via simple wf. commands.
wondyframe.overview: WondyFrame is a Discord bot built with discord.js for Warframe players, created by developer "wondyr". It focuses on one job: surfacing Warframe game information (especially prime/relic farming locations) directly in Discord so users don't have to search the wiki or multiple sites manually. Per its Discord Bot List page it runs in roughly 495 servers, uses the wf. command prefix, and currently supports PC world state only. The developer explicitly frames it as a no-frills utility: "This bot doesn't have any fancy functions or commands. It does one thing and that is giving you the information you need quickly."
keyFeatures: Prime item / relic farming lookup — type a prime part and get the relics plus best drop locations
Baro Ki'Teer merchant inventory and timer
Active sorties, invasions, and fissure missions
Nightwave challenges
Earth and Cetus day/night cycle timers
Orb Vallis and Cambion Drift weather (warm/cold, Vome/Fass) status
Simple wf. command prefix, discord.js based
Work-in-progress item command
wondyframe.bestFor: Warframe Discord communities on PC who want a lightweight in-server bot for quick relic/farming lookups and world-state timers without leaving Discord.
wondyframe.sentimentSummary: No community discussion (Reddit, YouTube, forums) was found within the available research, so there is no measurable public sentiment. The only signal is its adoption of ~495 servers listed on Discord Bot List and a self-described minimalist feature set. It appears to be a small, single-developer project rather than a widely-discussed community staple.

safety.notes: Read-only Discord bot that surfaces public Warframe world-state/drop data as chat responses. It does not read game memory, overlay the client, require Warframe account credentials, or facilitate real-money trading, so it poses no account-risk or DE-ToS concerns. Standard Discord bot caution applies (grant only the permissions it needs).

wondyframe.tierReason: Modest adoption (~495 servers), single-developer project, PC-only, and no discoverable community reviews or discussion. It is a functional but small-audience utility rather than a broadly-loved or widely-used bot, and larger competitors (e.g. established Warframe Discord bots) dominate the space.
pros: Simple, focused single-purpose design — quick relic/farming lookups without wiki hunting
Covers the common world-state needs: Baro, sorties, invasions, fissures, Nightwave, cycles, weather
Runs in ~495 servers, so it is a real working bot with some adoption
Free and easy to add via Discord Bot List

Item command is work-in-progress / incomplete
Single-developer project with no visible reviews, ratings, or active community discussion
0 recent upvotes on Discord Bot List suggests low current momentum

warbot.oneLiner: An abandoned 2018 hobby Discord bot that surfaces Warframe item stats, warframe.market prices, and world-state timers via text commands.
warbot.overview: WarBot (by GitHub user PYROP3) is a Discord bot written in JavaScript on the discord.io library that lets players query Warframe game data from within a Discord server. It supports item lookups via bracket notation (e.g. [soma]), customizable stat tracking, average price checks over the last day from warframe.market, and in-game event timers such as Cetus day/night, Baro Ki'Teer arrivals, void fissures, and sorties. The project is effectively abandoned: the repository has 0 stars, 0 forks, 13 total commits, and no activity since August 2018, with some features (alerts, sorties) noted as experimental/incomplete. There is no public invite listing, and it must be self-hosted from source.
keyFeatures: Item lookup with bracket notation (e.g. [soma])
warframe.market average price checking (last ~24h)
Customizable attribute tracking (health, shields, components, etc.)
World-state timers: Cetus day/night cycle, Baro Ki'Teer arrival
Alerts, void fissures, and sorties (marked experimental/incomplete)
Attribute-specific stat queries (e.g. a warframe's shields)
Shortened/first-letter command abbreviations
Self-hosted Discord bot built on discord.io (JavaScript)
warbot.bestFor: Essentially no one today; only of interest to a developer curious about a small 2018 Warframe Discord bot code sample to fork or learn from. General players should use a maintained bot instead.
warbot.sentimentSummary: There is no discoverable community sentiment for this specific tool. It has no Reddit threads, YouTube videos, Trustpilot page, bot-list votes, or blog coverage, and its GitHub repo has zero stars and zero forks. The apparent r/Warbot subreddit and Warframe "Pyrol" posts found in searches are unrelated to this project. In short, it appears to be a personal/learning project that never gained an audience.

safety.notes: Read-only Discord bot that pulls public game data and warframe.market prices; it does not read game memory or use an overlay, and requires no Warframe account credentials, so it poses no DE ToS/EULA risk. The practical concern is not safety but abandonment: no updates since August 2018 means the discord.io library and API integrations are likely broken or deprecated, and self-hosting unmaintained code is on you.

warbot.tierReason: Abandoned since 2018 with zero stars/forks, no public invite, and features flagged as incomplete. Not because it is dangerous, but because it is dead and superseded by maintained alternatives.

Covers the useful basics in one bot: item stats, market prices, and world-state timers

odealo.oneLiner: A real-money player-to-player marketplace where users buy and sell Warframe platinum, items, and gear for cash — a form of RMT that violates Warframe's rules.
odealo.overview: Odealo is a third-party, escrow-based marketplace that lets people trade Warframe in-game assets for real money. Sellers list platinum, Prime parts, mods, weapons/Warframes, Kuva, Archon Shards, Forma, and cosmetics, and buyers pay in cash-backed "Credits" (1:1 USD) via PayPal, Skrill, or Webmoney, with Odealo holding funds in escrow until delivery is confirmed. It is not an official or DE-affiliated service; it is a general MMO currency/item marketplace that covers many games, with a dedicated Warframe section. Buying platinum or items for real money from a third party is real-money trading (RMT), which is against Warframe's EULA/Terms of Service and can result in account action from Digital Extremes.
keyFeatures: Player-to-player marketplace for Warframe platinum, Prime parts, mods, weapons/Warframes, Kuva, Archon Shards, Forma, and cosmetics
Real-money transactions using cash-backed Credits (1:1 with USD)
Escrow system holding payment until the buyer confirms in-game delivery
Payment via PayPal, Skrill, and Webmoney
Seller ratings, feedback, and verification levels
Free listings with prices set by competing sellers
Multi-game marketplace (Warframe is one of many titles covered)
odealo.bestFor: There is no safe recommended use case within Warframe's rules — it targets players who want to buy platinum/items with real cash, which is prohibited RMT. Legitimate players should use in-game trading or warframe.market instead.
odealo.sentimentSummary: Odealo itself carries broadly positive third-party merchant reviews (roughly 4 stars on Trustpilot across ~449 reviews, 4.3/5 on Sitejabber) as a functioning storefront, but that reflects transaction reliability, not community endorsement. Within the Warframe community, buying platinum or items for real money is widely regarded as RMT that undermines the trade economy and risks account bans, so the tool is generally viewed with suspicion or hostility rather than approval. Because platinum is cheap and easily farmed via in-game trading and warframe.market, most players see little legitimate reason to use it.

safety.notes: This is real-money trading (RMT). Buying Warframe platinum, items, or accounts with real cash from third parties violates Warframe's EULA/Terms of Service; Digital Extremes can suspend or ban accounts involved in RMT, and there is no in-game recovery for lost items or plat. Payment and account-sharing risks also exist on the buyer side. Not affiliated with or endorsed by Digital Extremes.

odealo.tierReason: Classified 'avoid' because it facilitates prohibited real-money trading that can get accounts banned, is not endorsed by DE, and duplicates functionality that is free, safe, and better served by in-game trading and warframe.market.
